essence: David asks what to add to his usual conversation-starter prompt to obtain a session code and resume a previous Claude Code session.

For the agentic OS David wants brain-stored North-Star guidance (patterns/anti-patterns/decisions) and weighs outside-world connectivity — ngrok tunnels vs a communication bus vs tying directly to a Supabase database — then asks for a post-compaction handover that preserves all learnings and checks whether combo conversations 1-3 completed or left open loops.

actions.text: Produce a handover message after compaction preserving patterns, anti-patterns, learnings and decisions, and verify via written artifacts and commit messages whether combos 1-3 are complete or have open loops

essence: David names his personal agentic OS 'Project Theodore' (inspired by the film Her) with Samantha as the executive-assistant persona (Ian has Samantha, Nick has Ghosty), plans a daily morning check-in to offset memory/stress challenges, sketches memory (Kevin), transcription/translation, and background agents that recover Claude session IDs from JSONL and summarize them, then plans his day: SupportSignal unit tests + incident capture till 13:00, Remotion 14:00-16:00, and outreach to Lars, Steve, Guy, Ian and a Thursday whiteboard session pitch to Shane.

actions.text: Run SupportSignal unit tests this morning, then incident capture until 13:00
Send invitation to Lars and talk to Steve about it
Contact Guy and talk to Ian about Project Theodore
Pitch Shane a Thursday-night whiteboard/conversation session on Project Theodore (library venue)
Have background agents locate Claude conversation JSONL files, extract session IDs, and summarize each so sessions can be selectively reloaded

essence: David coaches his voice-coach friend (Guy) to fix his 20 four-minute confidence videos by adding hook + scenario before the bullet-point body: feed all 20 scripts plus David's Loom transcript into ChatGPT as a seed prompt, generate scenario categories, then annotate each script with 2 scenarios and 3 hooks each (intro = hook + scenario, body, CTA) — while explicitly blocking Guy's perfectionist rabbit-hole research because the assignment is to ship videos, not perfect them.

actions.text: Open a new ChatGPT chat, paste the Loom transcript as seed context, generate scenario categories for the 20 scripts, then annotate each script with 2 scenarios and 3 hooks plus a suggested outro

essence: Cafe session with a developer friend where David commits to a daily-livestream distribution strategy: rename the 2.5k-sub appydave-research channel to the Coding Lab, livestream every work session to YouTube/Twitter/Facebook/LinkedIn (Twitter Pro paid), buy a new M4 as permanent workstation with the idle M2 as dedicated ClaudeBot machine, pay Guy (voice coach) a consistent monthly rate to cut streams with Jan polishing, and adopt the friend's 'token offloading' pattern — NotebookLM as topic-specific second brain queried via MCP so coding agents don't burn their own tokens.

actions.text: Rebrand appydave research channel to the Coding Lab and start daily livestreams across YouTube, Twitter, Facebook and LinkedIn
Engage Guy at a lower consistent monthly rate to cut livestream footage, with Jan doing polish, republished to the AppyDave channel
Re-familiarize with Remotion + the new Claude skill, make 4-5 sample videos, then hand the team 10 categories with 5 image prompts each for 10 Remotion videos

essence: David and Lars Filtenberg agree a ~3-month AI-workflow engagement starting March 1 (~$5k/month, weekly check-ins) with a smaller month-one prepayment David invoices now to buy an M4 (personal workstation) and repurpose his M2 as a ClaudeBot/OpenClaw machine, livestreaming and documenting both setups so Lars can model them without exposing his proprietary SEO logic — framed around David's librarian/guardrails pattern, council-of-agents skill idea, virtual employees replacing hires, and OpenClaw's security fiasco (open ports, naming/cease-and-desist, Cloudflare/Vercel $5 installs).

actions.text: Send Lars an invoice for the smaller month-one prepayment
Buy a new M4 Mac and set up the M2 as a ClaudeBot/OpenClaw machine, livestreaming both setup tracks as guidance for Lars
Kick off the 3-month engagement March 1 with at least one solid weekly meeting
Catch up with Lars Friday afternoon before he leaves Chiang Mai

essence: Alongside arranging a 2:30 bike-service pickup, a collaborator messages David from the farmer's market saying he is 'all in' on making videos and asks David to bring materials and instructions today or tomorrow so he can deliver the requested video content.

actions.text: Bring video materials and instructions to the collaborator today or tomorrow so he can start producing the videos

essence: Chiang Mai AI dev meetup (Jan present): Kimi 2.5 as near-frontier thinking partner (allegedly built on leaked Claude weights, swarm-pretrained), skills-vs-MCP abstraction debate, the 'use when' description heuristic plus listing skills as pointers in CLAUDE.md for ~100% invocation, a Vercel-style eval where a compressed agents.md beat pure skills, sub-agent frontmatter 'skills:' autoloading for David's librarian ontology docs, OpenClaw/ClaudeBot security fallout (open VPS ports, prompt injection, supply-chain risk), and the official Anthropic Claude Code event Feb 7 at CMU with lightning talks and free credits.

actions.text: Register for the official Anthropic Claude Code event on Feb 7 at CMU
Try sub-agent frontmatter skills autoloading so the librarian agent's ontology/topology docs only load when the librarian is active
